title: Metabolomics: Primary Metabolites by GC-TOF MS
author: Oliver Fiehn
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.64nhgve

steps.title: A General parameters for GC-TOF MS...
steps.instruction: A General parameters for GC-TOF MS profiling for primary metabolism Injection with Gerstel Cold Injection system and Agilent 7890A gas chromatograph: • Method Name: “quick_Splitless_ms_alex” • Injector: Gerstel MPS/CIS injector • Injector Mode: Splitless • Purge Time: 25 sec • Purge Flow: 40mL/min • Injector Vol: 0.5µL • Carrier gas: Helium • Carrier gas flow: 1ml/min • CIS Temp Program: • Gas chromatograph temperature program: • Gas chromatograph column: RTX-5MS, 30m long, 0.25mm i.d. 0.25mm film duraguard with 10m guard to column. (Restek order # 12723-127) Mass spectrometry parameters with Leco Pegasus III or IV: • Solvent Delay: 335 sec (varies with time, confirm with each column) • Ion Source Temp: 250oC • Scan Rate: 17specs/sec • Detector Voltage: 1750 V to 1950V (settings according to age of detector)

steps.title: Cool oven to 30°C a
steps.instruction: Cool oven to 30°C a. Press Oven on the front of the Agilent GC b. Enter 30 on keypad

steps.title: Cool inlet (CIS temp) to 20°C a
steps.instruction: Cool inlet (CIS temp) to 20°C a. From Maestro, click Method b. Click Load c. Select 20C for injector plate change and clean method d. Click Get Ready

steps.title: Turn the electrical OFF by moving...
steps.instruction: Turn the electrical OFF by moving the switch, located on the front of the instrument, to the position

steps.title: Wait approximately 1
steps.instruction: Wait approximately 1.5 hours until the ion source temperature is < 100°C

steps.title: Close both the Maestro and ChromaTOF softwares
steps.instruction: Close both the Maestro and ChromaTOF softwares

steps.title: Turn the vacuum OFF by moving...
steps.instruction: Turn the vacuum OFF by moving the switch, located on the front of the instrument, to the position

steps.title: Slowly vent the turbo pump by...
steps.instruction: Slowly vent the turbo pump by carefully twisting the vent knob until the “hissing” noise stops.

steps.title: Close the vent knob
steps.instruction: Close the vent knob.

steps.title: Unscrew the pump cable (no pump cable for CORE)
steps.instruction: Unscrew the pump cable (no pump cable for CORE)

steps.title: Remove the safety pin from the...
steps.instruction: Remove the safety pin from the table and carefully separate the table

steps.title: Open the oven door To install column at injector site:
steps.instruction: Open the oven door To install column at injector site:

steps.title: Remove the old column from the...
steps.instruction: Remove the old column from the injector end and insert new column in following the laboratory protocol Cutting the Column a. NOTE: On the new column, “Guard Column End” goes to the injector. “Guard” always refers to injector end! b. NOTE: If reusing the ferrule or adapter, sonicate in ethyl acetate for 5 minutes to clean To install column at detector site:

steps.title: Inside the oven, unscrew the round...
steps.instruction: Inside the oven, unscrew the round plate (facing out) to reveal the transfer line and old column (CORE GC does not have this cover, transfer line is in plain sight)

steps.title: Unscrew the transfer line nut and...
steps.instruction: Unscrew the transfer line nut and carefully pull the old column out of the detector end

steps.title: Break off the nut (by breaking...
steps.instruction: Break off the nut (by breaking the old column) and remove the grey ferrule from within a. NOTE: Use a paperclip/tack to remove ferrule from the nut if it’s stuck. If it can’t be removed, use a new nut.

steps.title: Unwind about 2 feet of the...
steps.instruction: Unwind about 2 feet of the new column and insert the grey ferrule and nut

steps.title: Cut about 10 cm of the...
steps.instruction: Cut about 10 cm of the new column and wipe clean with a Kimwipe and methanol

steps.title: Feed the new column through the...
steps.instruction: Feed the new column through the interface pole with the transfer nut lightly (finger tight) screwed on, leaving about 4-6mm sticking out the other end

steps.title: Using a Sharpie, and keeping 4-6mm...
steps.instruction: Using a Sharpie, and keeping 4-6mm sticking out of the end of the interface pole, mark the column position on the opposite side containing the nut and ferrule

steps.title: Remove the column from the interface...
steps.instruction: Remove the column from the interface pole and feed the column into the actual interface, screw in the nut and ferrule VERY TIGHTLY and make sure column is the correct length based on the Sharpie mark. a. Ensure threading on nut is good, you should be able to screw in the nut almost all the way by hand, and tighten with wrench at the very end; otherwise, select a more appropriate nut.

steps.title: Inside the oven, screw the round...
steps.instruction: Inside the oven, screw the round silver cover back into place (skip for CORE GC)

steps.title: Carefully push back the table, replacing...
steps.instruction: Carefully push back the table, replacing the safety pin; close oven door.

steps.title: Screw the pump cable back on...
steps.instruction: Screw the pump cable back on the turbo pump (skip for CORE GC)

steps.title: Turn the vacuum ON (opposite of
steps.instruction: Turn the vacuum ON (opposite of

steps.title: Open both the Maestro and ChromaTOF software
steps.instruction: Open both the Maestro and ChromaTOF software

steps.title: Wait until the vacuum pressure reaches...
steps.instruction: Wait until the vacuum pressure reaches 1E-7; takes at least 3 hours (pressure reading is located on the bottom-right corner of the LECO software)

steps.title: Turn the electrical ON (opposite of
steps.instruction: Turn the electrical ON (opposite of

steps.title: After transfer line reaches 280°C and...
steps.instruction: After transfer line reaches 280°C and the ion source reaches 250°C, let instrument sit for another hour (to reduce moisture in system) before conditioning column.

steps.title: Condition the column twice using the...
steps.instruction: Condition the column twice using the following temperature program: Initial temp: 50°C Initial time: 1 minute Rate

steps.title: 10°C/minute Final temp
steps.instruction: 10°C/minute Final temp

steps.title: 100°C Final time 1:10 minutes Rate
steps.instruction: 100°C Final time 1:10 minutes Rate

steps.title: 10°C/minute Final temp
steps.instruction: 10°C/minute Final temp

steps.title: 330°C Final time
steps.instruction: 330°C Final time

steps.title: At this time, also adjust CIS...
steps.instruction: At this time, also adjust CIS temp to 250°C for at least 15 minutes before bringing it down to 50°C (change temperatures by loading liner condition or sample run method).

steps.title: Perform a leak check by following...
steps.instruction: Perform a leak check by following the laboratory protocol performing a Leak Check .

steps.title: Condition the column with corn samples...
steps.instruction: Condition the column with corn samples and quality controls, following the laboratory protocol conditioning the Column. B2 How to cut the Gas Chromatography capillary column after analysis of complex biological matrices to restore the system. Method:

steps.title: Cool oven to 30°C
steps.instruction: Cool oven to 30°C. a. Press Oven on the front of the Agilent GC. b. Using the keypad, type 30 , press Enter .

steps.title: Cool the inlet to 20°C
steps.instruction: Cool the inlet to 20°C. a. On Maestro software, click Method → Load. b. Select 20C for injector plate change and clean method and hit “ Get Ready” button.*

steps.title: Turn gas saver OFF
steps.instruction: Turn gas saver OFF. a. Press Back Inlet on front of GC. b. Scroll down till Gas Saver, press OFF on keypad.

steps.title: Open the oven door
steps.instruction: Open the oven door. Use wrench “I” to unscrew and remove the adapter nut.

steps.title: Remove the column by pulling on the ferrule by hand
steps.instruction: Remove the column by pulling on the ferrule by hand. If successful, skip to

steps.title: a. Note
steps.instruction: a. Note: If the ferrule is stuck, you will need to remove the back adapter (steps 6-

steps.title: and try to pry it off the adapter with pliers
steps.instruction: and try to pry it off the adapter with pliers. If the ferrule becomes unusable, you will need to use a new ferrule and follow the guidelines in steps 9b, 12a, and 19a.

steps.title: Insert the septa (minimize air exposure...
steps.instruction: Insert the septa (minimize air exposure inside column) to the end of the column.

steps.title: Remove the round metal plate covering adapter
steps.instruction: Remove the round metal plate covering adapter.

steps.title: Use wrench “G” to unscrew the...
steps.instruction: Use wrench “G” to unscrew the adapter, and replace with a new adapter.* Replace metal cover as well. a. Do not over-tighten adapter, adapter should go on easily by hand, only use wrench at the very end to tighten (finger-tight).

steps.title: Measure approximately 10 cm and cut...
steps.instruction: Measure approximately 10 cm and cut the column with the Agilent ruler/cutter. a. If ferrule is undamaged/relatively new, and still fits snugly around column, simply pull it down further along column before making the cut. b. If ferrule is damaged/ unusable, replace with new ferrule before cutting column.*

steps.title: Examine the new cut with the...
steps.instruction: Examine the new cut with the magnifying glass to ensure a clean, straight cut. a. If inadequate, make another cut a few mm down.

steps.title: Clean the tip of the column...
steps.instruction: Clean the tip of the column with MeOH on a kimwipe.

steps.title: Using a ruler, measure the column...
steps.instruction: Using a ruler, measure the column sticking out from the front of the ferrule out to 1.7 cm. a. If you have to use a new ferrule, it will be very loose. Tighten first by installing column back into injector, and then removing it to measure 1.7cm. Ferrule should snugly fit around column.*

steps.title: Carefully thread column back up into adapter
steps.instruction: Carefully thread column back up into adapter.

steps.title: Firmly push the ferrule into place by hand
steps.instruction: Firmly push the ferrule into place by hand.

steps.title: Screw the adapter nut back into place
steps.instruction: Screw the adapter nut back into place.

steps.title: Position the column so that no...
steps.instruction: Position the column so that no part of it is touching the sides of the oven—to avoid uneven heating of column.

steps.title: Close the oven door
steps.instruction: Close the oven door.

steps.title: Turn the gas saver back ON
steps.instruction: Turn the gas saver back ON. Check if pressure holds.

steps.title: Increase the oven temperature back to 50°C
steps.instruction: Increase the oven temperature back to 50°C. a. If new ferrule is installed, increase CIS temp to 250C (load Maestro liner condition method) for ten minutes, and bring it back down to 50C (load Maestro sample run method)

steps.title: Perform a leak check by following...
steps.instruction: Perform a leak check by following the laboratory protocol performing a Leak Check. Make sure CIS temp is at 50C, and pressure is at 7.7psi before running leak check. B3 How to clean and assemble liners for Gerstel CIS injector system Method

steps.title: Remove glass liner from transport adapter...
steps.instruction: Remove glass liner from transport adapter (liner heads) and dispose used glass liners in the broken glass waste container

steps.title: Unscrew smaller piece from larger head using an Allen wrench
steps.instruction: Unscrew smaller piece from larger head using an Allen wrench

steps.title: Remove the septa from smaller piece...
steps.instruction: Remove the septa from smaller piece using the push pin without scratching the surface of metal piece.

steps.title: Rinse inside of adapter head with...
steps.instruction: Rinse inside of adapter head with isopropanol, scrubbing through with the test tube brush. Take care not to squirt isopropanol directly on rubber O-rings (the rubber will dry out/become brittle over time).

steps.title: Smaller piece is sonicated for 5 minutes in isopropanol
steps.instruction: Smaller piece is sonicated for 5 minutes in isopropanol.

steps.title: Leave all pieces on Kimwipe to dry
steps.instruction: Leave all pieces on Kimwipe to dry

steps.title: When dry, insert a new septa...
steps.instruction: When dry, insert a new septa and screw in smaller piece into the adapter head using an Allen wrench

steps.title: Attach the adapter to a new...
steps.instruction: Attach the adapter to a new glass liner using the black assembly tool a. Insert a glass liner (baffled end first) into the assembly tool b. Place the assembled adapter onto the assembly tool (septa facing up) c. Press down on the top of the adapter a few times to firmly attach the liner to the adapter d. Pull out the newly assembled liner and tug gently on glass liner to make sure it is snugly attached to adapter head.

steps.title: Sign in to MiniX ( http://minix
steps.instruction: Sign in to MiniX ( http://minix.fiehnlab.ucdavis.edu/ )

steps.title: Click on list studies and select...
steps.instruction: Click on list studies and select study of interest (see LAB protocol for Creating a MiniX study)

steps.title: On left side menu list, select
steps.instruction: On left side menu list, select

steps.title: Under Binbase specific settings , a
steps.instruction: Under Binbase specific settings , a. select which instrument will be used: core GC-TOF –a or research GC-TOF –b . b. check all boxes; these are for blanks and QC6s, and the QC6-QC1 curve.

steps.title: Under sample name generation settings , a
steps.instruction: Under sample name generation settings , a. select date you will start, if unsure, estimate. b. the date you enter will serve as the first six digits of your filenames for the first 50 samples; if you have more than 50, the next 50 will have the date for the following day.

steps.title: Under data acquisition settings , a
steps.instruction: Under data acquisition settings , a. for research instrument, select the only option available for all three methods. b. for CORE instrument, there are no options available, so leave as is.

steps.title: When finished, click – it will...
steps.instruction: When finished, click – it will display the acquisition table you have just created.

steps.title: Click on view study
steps.instruction: Click on view study. Under Attachments, download the acquisition table onto GC-TOF computer by clicking C2 Creating a ChromaTOF sequence table from miniX study

steps.title: Transfer the downloaded acquisition file to...
steps.instruction: Transfer the downloaded acquisition file to minix folder located in gctof drive located on network jupiter.fiehnlab.ucdavis.edu a. save file by date, followed by miniX ID and brief description; if it is a CORE study, for example: CORE_120628_MX95240_rafael plants

steps.title: On ChromaTOF, in the Database window...
steps.instruction: On ChromaTOF, in the Database window (top left screen), click on Acquisition Tasks a. On menu bar (very top row), click Acquisition Tasks → Sequence Table → Load … b. Select file of interest and open to load file onto Acquisition Tasks screen.

steps.title: The beginning of each sequence starts...
steps.instruction: The beginning of each sequence starts with three liner conditions, followed by a reagent blank and QC6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 in this order: a. Leave all other columns blank. By default, Type and Tray columns are automatically filled. Type is always “Sample”, and Tray contains unique minix number for each sample. Leave Tray cells empty for liner conditions or any other non-minix samples. b. To fill in columns, highlight column of interest, or cell, for example, right-click → Fill → quick-splitless_working_PT 25 sec → OK c. Click on the “+” sign to add a row above selected row or the “– “ to delete selected row(s). Multiple rows can be deleted, but rows can only be added manually d. Leave vial 2 empty, and samples start at vial 10 e. Liner conditioning occurs every 10 samples i. Insert 5 additional rows and copy and paste the Tray, Vial, and Name of the 3 liner conditions, the reagent blank and QC

steps.title: f
steps.instruction: f. End the entire study with a single QC6 run. g. Note that the tray only holds 98 vials. If you have more than this number of vials, you need to restart numbering at #10 after #

steps.title: Re-save this newly modified sequence by...
steps.instruction: Re-save this newly modified sequence by overwriting the existing file in miniX folder: Acquisition Tasks → Sequence Table → Save … CORE_120628_MX95240_rafael plants

steps.title: Copy the entire column containing the...
steps.instruction: Copy the entire column containing the vial numbers into an Excel spreadsheet (used to create the Maestro sequence). Note: Creating a ChromaTOF sequence from a non-miniX study

steps.title: Follow steps 12-14 from above using...
steps.instruction: Follow steps 12-14 from above using the “+” icon to add lines. C3 Creating a Maestro sequence from ChromaTOF sequence

steps.title: On Maestro menu bar click Sequence → New … a
steps.instruction: On Maestro menu bar click Sequence → New … a. Or use an existing template, but first Sequence → Save As a new sequence file before proceeding. b. Click on or Sequence → Edit to open up Maestro sequence table for editing.

steps.title: On ChromaTOF, highlight entire Vial column, and copy Ctrl+C
steps.instruction: On ChromaTOF, highlight entire Vial column, and copy Ctrl+C.

steps.title: On Maestro, highlight first cell of...
steps.instruction: On Maestro, highlight first cell of Vial column (entire cell should be completely shaded blue), and paste Ctrl+V . a. Do not worry about having the correct number of lines; Maestro will add lines as necessary. b. Leave screen alone even if it looks like it is going to freeze after you paste; sometimes it does that with a brand new sequence. Just give software some time.

steps.title: To add rows
steps.instruction: To add rows: select a row and click the Insert icon at the top of the screen; this inserts a new row above highlighted row.

steps.title: Add a liner change (NEWLINER) before...
steps.instruction: Add a liner change (NEWLINER) before every liner conditioning set, and fill in all other necessary information as follows:

steps.title: To check and confirm sequences on...
steps.instruction: To check and confirm sequences on ChromaTOF and Maestro match: a. Highlight the entire sequence table by clicking the top-left corner of the sequence table, and copy/paste to Excel spreadsheet. b. Delete the newliner positions in the Maestro. c. Confirm vial numbers in both columns (LECO and Maestro) match.

steps.title: Click OK on the Maestro sequence table
steps.instruction: Click OK on the Maestro sequence table. a. You will lose changes made if you close sequence table window without clicking OK .

steps.title: Save the sequence
steps.instruction: Save the sequence: Sequence → Save As … and save under C:\Program files\Maestro\1\Sequence using the same name you saved sequence in ChromaTOF.
